name: code2world-gui-model
description: Predict next GUI state via renderable code generation. Generate HTML representations of mobile screens. Train vision-language models on GUI trajectories. Enable action-conditioned UI prediction. Build world models for GUI agents.
paperTitle: Code2World: A GUI World Model via Renderable Code Generation
content:
# Code2World: A GUI World Model via Renderable Code Generation ## Core Insight GUI agents need to predict next visual state before executing actions—a capability essential for planning and verification. Existing pixel-based approaches sacrifice structural control; text-based representations lack visual fidelity. Code2World generates next UI state as renderable HTML code, capturing both high-fidelity visuals and fine-grained structural semantics. Trained via cold-start SFT then render-aware RL, Code2World-8B outperforms GPT-5 and Gemini-3-Pro on UI prediction benchmarks and boosts downstream navigation by +9.5%, enabled by a synthesized 80K AndroidCode dataset of HTML-action pairs. ## Implementation ```python import json from typing import Dict, List, Tuple, Optional from dataclasses import dataclass import numpy as np @dataclass class UIState: """Representation of a GUI screen.""" screen_id: str html_code: str action_description: str visual_description: str bounding_boxes: Dict[str, List[int]] # Element ID -> [x, y, w, h] @dataclass class Prediction: """Next UI state prediction.""" predicted_html: str confidence: float structural_score: float visual_score: float rendered_pixels: Optional[np.ndarray] = None class Code2WorldModel: """Vision-language coder for GUI world modeling.""" def __init__(self, max_html_len: int = 2048, vision_backbone: str = "clip"): self.max_html_len = max_html_len self.vision_backbone = vision_backbone self.html_tokenizer = SimpleHTMLTokenizer() self.rendering_engine = HTMLRenderer() def predict_next_state( self, current_screenshot: np.ndarray, current_html: str, action: str, use_rl_reward: bool = True ) -> Prediction: """ Predict next UI state given current state and action. Args: current_screenshot: Pixel representation of current screen current_html: HTML representation of current screen action: Description of intended action (e.g., "click button at (100, 200)") use_rl_reward: Whether to apply render-aware RL reward signal Returns: Prediction with generated HTML and confidence scores """ # Phase 1: Vision-Language Encoding # Extract visual features from screenshot + action description visual_features = self._encode_screenshot(current_screenshot) action_embedding = self._encode_action(action) html_embedding = self._encode_html(current_html) # Concatenate: visual + action + current HTML context combined_context = np.concatenate([ visual_features, action_embedding, html_embedding ]) # Phase 2: HTML Generation # Cold-start SFT: Decoder generates HTML token-by-token predicted_tokens = self._generate_html_tokens( combined_context, max_length=self.max_html_len ) predicted_html = self.html_tokenizer.decode(predicted_tokens) # Phase 3: Render-Aware RL Reward structural_score = 1.0 # Placeholder visual_score = 1.0 if use_rl_reward: # Render predicted HTML to pixels rendered_pixels = self.rendering_engine.render_html(predicted_html) # Structural reward: Does HTML parse correctly and contain expected elements? structural_score = self._compute_structural_reward( predicted_html, current_html, action ) # Visual reward: Does rendered output match expected visual changes? visual_score = self._compute_visual_reward( rendered_pixels, current_screenshot, action ) # Combine scores confidence = (structural_score + visual_score) / 2.0 return Prediction( predicted_html=predicted_html, confidence=float(confidence), structural_score=float(structural_score), visual_score=float(visual_score), rendered_pixels=rendered_pixels if use_rl_reward else None ) def _encode_screenshot(self, screenshot: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray: """Encode screenshot to visual features via vision backbone.""" # Mock: extract CLIP-style features (batch, 768) batch_size = screenshot.shape[0] if len(screenshot.shape) > 2 else 1 return np.random.randn(768).astype(np.float32) def _encode_action(self, action: str) -> np.ndarray: """Encode action description to embedding.""" # Mock: Simple tokenization + embedding tokens = action.split() return np.random.randn(256).astype(np.float32) def _encode_html(self, html: str) -> np.ndarray: """Encode current HTML to contextual embedding.""" # Mock: HTML structure encoding return np.random.randn(512).astype(np.float32) def _generate_html_tokens( self, context: np.ndarray, max_length: int = 2048 ) -> List[int]: """Generate HTML tokens autoregressively.""" tokens = [101] # START token for step in range(max_length): # Predict next token given context + generated tokens next_token_logits = self._forward_decoder(context, tokens) next_token = np.argmax(next_token_logits) tokens.append(next_token) if next_token == 102: # END token break return tokens def _forward_decoder( self, context: np.ndarray, prev_tokens: List[int] ) -> np.ndarray: """Decoder forward pass: predict logits for next token.""" # Mock: return random logits (vocab_size,) return np.random.randn(30522) def _compute_structural_reward( self, predicted_html: str, current_html: str, action: str ) -> float: """ Compute structural fidelity reward. Measures: - Is HTML valid and parseable? - Are expected UI elements present? - Does structure reflect action intent? """ reward = 0.0 # Check validity try: from html.parser import HTMLParser HTMLParser().feed(predicted_html) reward += 0.3 except: return 0.0 # Check element consistency current_elements = self._extract_elements(current_html) predicted_elements = self._extract_elements(predicted_html) element_iou = len(set(current_elements) & set(predicted_elements)) / len( set(current_elements) | set(predicted_elements) ) reward += 0.35 * element_iou # Check if action is reflected action_keywords = action.lower().split() html_contains_action = any(kw in predicted_html.lower() for kw in action_keywords) reward += 0.35 * float(html_contains_action) return reward def _compute_visual_reward( self, rendered_pixels: np.ndarray, current_pixels: np.ndarray, action: str ) -> float: """ Compute visual fidelity reward via render output. Measures: - Pixel-level consistency (SSIM, LPIPS) - Action-conditioned changes (e.g., button highlight) - Absence of artifacts """ # Mock: Structural Similarity (SSIM) between rendered and expected ssim_score = self._compute_ssim(rendered_pixels, current_pixels) # Action should cause visible change pixel_change = np.mean(np.abs(rendered_pixels - current_pixels)) change_reward = 1.0 if pixel_change > 0.05 else 0.5 return 0.7 * ssim_score + 0.3 * change_reward def _extract_elements(self, html: str) -> List[str]: """Extract UI elements from HTML.""" elements = [] for tag in ["button", "input", "span", "div", "a"]: count = html.count(f"<{tag}") if count > 0: elements.append(tag) return elements def _compute_ssim(self, img1: np.ndarray, img2: np.ndarray) -> float: """Compute Structural Similarity Index.""" # Mock SSIM (0-1 score) return 0.85 if np.random.random() > 0.2 else 0.65 class SimpleHTMLTokenizer: """Tokenizer for HTML code.""" def __init__(self, vocab_size: int = 30522): self.vocab_size = vocab_size def encode(self, html: str) -> List[int]: """Encode HTML string to tokens.""" return [hash(c) % self.vocab_size for c in html[:100]] def decode(self, tokens: List[int]) -> str: """Decode tokens to HTML string.""" # Simple mock: reconstruct basic HTML html_parts = [ '', '', '
', '', '
', '' ] return ''.join(html_parts) class HTMLRenderer: """Render HTML to pixel representation.""" def render_html(self, html: str) -> np.ndarray: """Render HTML to pixels (mock).""" # Return synthetic rendered image (480, 800, 3) return np.random.randint(0, 256, size=(480, 800, 3), dtype=np.uint8) class AndroidCodeDataset: """Synthesized dataset of Android GUI trajectories as HTML.""" def __init__(self, size: int = 80000): self.size = size self.examples = self._generate_dataset() def _generate_dataset(self) -> List[Dict]: """Generate synthetic HTML-action-screenshot pairs.""" examples = [] for i in range(self.size): example = { "id": f"android_code_{i:06d}", "screenshot_before": np.random.randint(0, 256, (480, 800, 3), dtype=np.uint8), "html_before": self._generate_html_state(i), "action": self._generate_action(i), "screenshot_after": np.random.randint(0, 256, (480, 800, 3), dtype=np.uint8), "html_after": self._generate_html_state(i + 1), } examples.append(example) return examples def _generate_html_state(self, seed: int) -> str: """Generate synthetic HTML UI state.""" np.random.seed(seed % 1000) buttons = np.random.randint(1, 4) inputs = np.random.randint(0, 2) html = '' for j in range(buttons): html += f'' for j in range(inputs): html += f'' html += '' return html def _generate_action(self, seed: int) -> str: """Generate synthetic action description.""" actions = [ "click button at (100, 200)", "type 'hello' in text field", "swipe left", "scroll down", "double-tap at (150, 300)" ] return actions[seed % len(actions)] class NavigationEvaluator: """Evaluate downstream GUI navigation with Code2World.""" def evaluate_navigation( self, model: Code2WorldModel, test_trajectories: List[List[Dict]], num_steps: int = 10 ) -> Dict: """Evaluate navigation success rate using world model predictions.""" successes = 0 total = 0 for trajectory in test_trajectories: for step in range(min(num_steps, len(trajectory) - 1)): current = trajectory[step] next_real = trajectory[step + 1] # Predict next state prediction = model.predict_next_state( current["screenshot"], current["html"], current["action"] ) # Check if prediction matches actual next state if prediction.confidence > 0.7: successes += 1 total += 1 success_rate = successes / max(1, total) return { "success_rate": float(success_rate), "improvement_over_baseline": 0.095, # +9.5% vs Gemini-2.5-Flash "num_trajectories_evaluated": len(test_trajectories), "num_steps": total } ``` ## Practical Guidance **Cold-Start SFT**: Begin with supervised fine-tuning on AndroidCode dataset. Train vision-language model to generate HTML conditioned on (screenshot, action, current_html). Use standard cross-entropy loss on token prediction. **Render-Aware RL**: After SFT convergence (2-3 epochs), switch to RL with composite reward: 0.7 * structural_score + 0.3 * visual_score. Structural score checks HTML validity and element preservation. Visual score compares rendered output to expected changes via SSIM. **Dataset Synthesis**: Translate GUI trajectories (screenshots + action sequences) into HTML via visual-feedback revision. For each screen, reverse-engineer HTML from pixel layout, then refine iteratively. Target 80K high-quality screen-action pairs across diverse Android apps. **Element Consistency**: Preserve UI elements across transitions (buttons, input fields, dialogs). When action modifies state (e.g., button click), update element visibility/properties in HTML. Avoid hallucinating new elements unrelated to action. **Rendering Backend**: Use lightweight HTML renderer (e.g., Chromium headless) to validate predictions. Rendered output should match expected screen changes with >0.8 SSIM. **Downstream Integration**: Plug Code2World into GUI agent planning. Before executing action, predict next state; use prediction for lookahead planning, error recovery, and verification. ## Reference - Code2World paper: [arXiv 2602.09856](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.09856) - GitHub repository: [AMAP-ML/Code2World](https://github.com/AMAP-ML/Code2World) - Project site: [Code2World Homepage](https://amap-ml.github.io/Code2World/) - AndroidCode dataset: 80K+ synthetic HTML-action pairs - Performance: Rivaling GPT-5 and Gemini-3-Pro-Image on UI prediction; +9.5% navigation boost on downstream tasks
